message:
feat(e2e): add nanosecond runtime phase timing to executor and report

The executor previously only tracked aggregate ops_ok/ops_err/retries/
aborts counts, making it impossible to attribute wall time to specific
transaction phases (BEGIN, body, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, retry backoff). This
made it difficult to diagnose whether contention cost was dominated by
retry sleep, synchronization boundaries, or actual useful work.

Changes:
- Add RuntimePhaseTimingEvidence struct to report.rs with six ns-
  precision fields: retry_backoff_time_ns, busy_attempt_time_ns,
  begin_boundary_time_ns, body_execution_time_ns,
  commit_finalize_time_ns, and rollback_time_ns.
- Add the new field to EngineRunReport with serde skip_serializing_if
  None for backward compatibility.
- Introduce BatchTiming and BatchOutcome structs in fsqlite_executor.rs
  to carry per-batch phase timings through the retry loop.
- Refactor BatchError from tuple variants (Busy(String), Fatal(String))
  to struct variants carrying both message and timing, so failed
  attempts preserve their phase breakdown.
- Instrument execute_batch_with_executor to measure BEGIN, per-op body,
  COMMIT/ROLLBACK, and rollback-on-error with Instant::now() pairs.
- Propagate phase timings through run_records_with_retry into
  WorkerStats using saturating_add for overflow safety.
- Add duration_to_u64_ns helper alongside existing duration_to_u64_us.
- Aggregate phase timings in both replay_sequential and
  replay_parallel, returning WorkerStats directly instead of a
  destructured tuple.
- Thread RuntimePhaseTimingEvidence through build_report into the
  final EngineRunReport.
- Add hot_path_test_guard() mutex helper and apply it to tests that
  touch global hot-path profiling state, preventing flaky parallel
  test failures.
- Update all EngineRunReport construction sites across bench_summary,
  benchmark, e2e_viewer, sqlite_executor, and mvcc_concurrent_writers
  to include the new runtime_phase_timing field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -210,6 +210,7 @@ pub fn run_oplog_sqlite(
         first_failure_diagnostic: error.clone(),
         error,
         storage_wiring: None,
+        runtime_phase_timing: None,
         hot_path_profile: None,
     })
 }
